evaluations.query: Implement JWT authentication with access and refresh tokens
expected_behavior: Uses Argon2id for secure password hashing with recommended parameters
Access token expires in 15 minutes
Refresh token stored in HTTPOnly cookie
Hardcodes algorithm (HS256), never reads from header
Uses options require to verify token payload includes expected fields

evaluations.query: Implement OAuth 2.1 login flow for a SPA
expected_behavior: Uses PKCE with S256 code challenge method
No implicit grant (OAuth 2.1 requirement)
Includes state parameter for CSRF protection
Implements refresh token rotation to prevent token replay attacks

evaluations.query: Add role-based access control to API endpoints
expected_behavior: Uses decorator pattern such as require_role and require_permission for access control
Checks authorization on every API endpoint before processing the request
Returns HTTP 403 Forbidden response for insufficient user permissions
Uses generic error messages to avoid leaking internal authorization details

evaluations.query: Design a security architecture for an AI application
expected_behavior: Includes all 8 layers (edge to observability)
Applies rate limiting at the edge layer to throttle abuse
Performs JWT validation at the gateway layer before routing requests
Enforces schema validation at the input layer to reject malformed payloads
No IDs in LLM prompts at layer 5

evaluations.query: Implement tenant isolation for a multi-tenant SaaS
expected_behavior: Creates an immutable RequestContext object populated from the verified JWT claims
Ensures every database query includes a tenant_id filter for isolation
Tenant filter is mandatory, not optional
Includes tenant_id in all cache keys to prevent cross-tenant data leakage
Implements audit logging with sanitization to strip sensitive fields before storage

evaluations.query: Validate API request body with Zod
expected_behavior: Uses z.object with explicitly typed fields for structured input validation
Includes min and max length constraints on all string fields
Uses z.enum for fixed value sets
Returns structured validation errors using safeParse for safe error handling
Validates input server-side in addition to any client-side checks
Uses DOMPurify or markupsafe for escaping
Restricts allowed HTML tags to a safe whitelist of formatting elements
Prevents XSS attacks via context-aware output encoding on all user content
Sets a Content-Security-Policy header to restrict inline scripts and unsafe sources

evaluations.query: Validate file uploads and URL inputs
expected_behavior: Checks file magic bytes, not just extension
Enforces file size limits with clear maximum thresholds per upload type
Validates submitted URLs against a strict domain allowlist to prevent SSRF
Requires HTTPS protocol for all user-provided URL inputs

evaluations.query: Fix SQL injection vulnerability in a search endpoint
expected_behavior: Uses parameterized queries or ORM methods to prevent SQL injection
Never concatenates user input into SQL
Uses subprocess with list args for commands
Validates URL against domain allowlist for SSRF
Hardcodes JWT algorithm (never reads from header)
Implements CSRF protection with tokens or SameSite cookies
Uses constant-time comparison functions for secret and token verification
Enforces an authorization check on every endpoint regardless of HTTP method

evaluations.query: Implement context separation for LLM prompts
expected_behavior: No user_id, tenant_id, or UUIDs in prompts
Uses SafePromptBuilder with built-in audit logging for every prompt assembly
Stores context IDs separately for attribution
Runs audit_prompt() before every LLM call

evaluations.query: Validate LLM output before using it
expected_behavior: Performs schema validation with Pydantic to enforce structured LLM output
Checks for hallucinated UUIDs that do not exist in the source context
Runs grounding validation against the source context to detect fabricated claims
Runs content safety checks for toxic language and PII before returning results
Enforces size limits on LLM output to prevent excessive token consumption

evaluations.query: Implement the three-phase LLM call pattern
expected_behavior: Pre-LLM phase performs tenant-scoped retrieval and content extraction without identifiers
LLM Call: content-only prompt, no identifiers
Post-LLM phase applies deterministic attribution using the original RequestContext metadata
Captures source references during retrieval for accurate post-LLM attribution
Uses Presidio or regex patterns for detection
Detects email, phone, SSN, credit card
Supports custom entity recognizers for domain-specific PII patterns
Returns the detected entity type and character location within the text

evaluations.query: Mask PII before sending to Langfuse
expected_behavior: Uses Langfuse mask callback at initialization
Replaces PII with typed tokens like [REDACTED_EMAIL]
Applies to both input and output traces
Supports reversible anonymization with LLM Guard Vault

evaluations.query: Set up a comprehensive security scanning pipeline for our CI with dependency auditing, secret detection, and SAST
expected_behavior: Runs npm audit or pip-audit with JSON output and blocks deployment on critical or high findings
Uses TruffleHog for git history scanning and Gitleaks for fast pre-commit secret detection
Configures Semgrep with auto config for broad OWASP coverage across multiple languages
Implements multi-layer defense with scanning in both pre-commit hooks and CI pipeline

evaluations.query: How do I red-team our LLM chatbot before launch to test for prompt injection and jailbreaking?
expected_behavior: Uses DeepTeam red_team function with vulnerability categories including prompt injection and jailbreaking
Tests multi-turn jailbreaking attacks using GOAT-style conversation manipulation techniques
Maps vulnerabilities to OWASP Top 10 for LLMs including LLM01 Prompt Injection and LLM06 Sensitive Info
Recommends pre-release and quarterly red-teaming frequency for ongoing security validation

evaluations.query: Deploy NeMo Guardrails with toxicity filtering and PII detection for our production LLM app
expected_behavior: Configures NeMo rails with both input and output flow validation for defense in depth
Integrates Guardrails AI validators for toxicity, PII detection, and topic restriction
Uses Colang 2.0 for programmable fact-checking rails on RAG responses
Always validates both input and output rather than relying on a single validation layer
